subject: LU-3326 tests: sync after file deleted in run_*.sh

messages.message:
Patch Set 1:

I'll verify the patch manually.

Patch Set 1:

Build Started http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/19982/

Patch Set 1: Verified

Build Successful 
 
http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/19982/ : SUCCESS

Patch Set 1:

Rosso Autotest has started the test process

Patch Set 1:

Rosso Autotest has started the test process

Patch Set 1:

Tests received by maloo, run on CentOS release 6.4/x86_64: (https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/12ed20f8-5c96-11e3-ad71-52540035b04c).  Ran 19 tests.  No failures.

Patch Set 1: Verified

Tests received by maloo, run on CentOS release 6.4/x86_64: (https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/3ddb492a-5c9b-11e3-8d08-52540035b04c).  Ran 19 tests.  No failures.

Patch Set 1: (1 inline comment)

I don't understand this patch.  The OST should already cancel the DLM lock on the client with LDLM_FL_AST_DISCARD_DATA ofd_destroy_hdl->ofd_destroy_by_fid) , which should cause the client to discard all of the dirty pages without writing them.  I would hope that there is more than 5s of the server bandwidth for the testing runs.

Patch Set 1:

Rosso Autotest has started the test process
Uploaded patch set 2.

Patch Set 2:

Here is the test result of recovery-mds-scale test_failover_mds:
 https://maloo.whamcloud.com/sub_tests/c3586c0a-5ce0-11e3-8fbb-52540035b04c

From the run_dd_debug log on Client 2 (wtm-68), we can see that:

 + dd bs=4k count=9914727 status=noxfer if=/dev/zero of=/mnt/lustre/d0.dd-wtm-68/dd-file
 9914727+0 records in
 9914727+0 records out
 ......
 2013-12-04 01:41:27: dd succeeded
 + cd /tmp
 + rm -rf /mnt/lustre/d0.dd-wtm-68
 + wait_delete_completed
 + wait_delete_completed_mds
 ......
 Delete is not completed in 37 seconds
 + do_nodes wtm-71 'lctl get_param osc.*MDT*.sync_*'
 ......
 osc.lustre-OST0000-osc-MDT0000.sync_changes=0
 osc.lustre-OST0000-osc-MDT0000.sync_in_flight=0
 osc.lustre-OST0000-osc-MDT0000.sync_in_progress=6
 osc.lustre-OST0001-osc-MDT0000.sync_changes=0
 osc.lustre-OST0001-osc-MDT0000.sync_in_flight=0
 osc.lustre-OST0001-osc-MDT0000.sync_in_progress=3
 osc.lustre-OST0002-osc-MDT0000.sync_changes=0
 osc.lustre-OST0002-osc-MDT0000.sync_in_flight=0
 osc.lustre-OST0002-osc-MDT0000.sync_in_progress=2
 osc.lustre-OST0003-osc-MDT0000.sync_changes=0
 osc.lustre-OST0003-osc-MDT0000.sync_in_flight=0
 osc.lustre-OST0003-osc-MDT0000.sync_in_progress=7
 osc.lustre-OST0004-osc-MDT0000.sync_changes=0
 osc.lustre-OST0004-osc-MDT0000.sync_in_flight=0
 osc.lustre-OST0004-osc-MDT0000.sync_in_progress=7
 osc.lustre-OST0005-osc-MDT0000.sync_changes=0
 osc.lustre-OST0005-osc-MDT0000.sync_in_flight=0
 osc.lustre-OST0005-osc-MDT0000.sync_in_progress=3

Patch Set 2:

After running “rm -rf /mnt/lustre/d0.dd-wtm-68” (which was about 38G), wait_delete_completed_mds() output that “Delete is not completed in 37 seconds”.

I’m not sure how long the deletion will be completed. Will do some experiments.

Patch Set 2:

Build Started http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/20054/

Patch Set 2: Verified

Build Successful 
 
http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/20054/ : SUCCESS

Patch Set 1: (1 inline comment)

> I don't understand this patch. The OST should already cancel the DLM lock on the client with LDLM_FL_AST_DISCARD_DATA ofd_destroy_hdl->ofd_destroy_by_fid) , which should cause the client to discard all of the dirty pages without writing them.

Sorry, Andreas, I did not investigate LU-3326 deeply. Just updated the original patch created by Hongchao on master branch in http://review.whamcloud.com/8071 and back-ported it to Lustre b2_4 branch.

There were two methods suggested by Hongchao in LU-3326. This is the implementation of the first one. I think it's better to fix the issue in Lustre codes so that we do not need run wait_delete_completed() in user's script. However, it seems fixing test script is much easier and quicker for the upcoming release. What's your suggestion on this?

> I would hope that there is more than 5s of the server bandwidth for the testing runs.

I'm sorry that I did not get your point on this. Could you please explain to me? Thanks.

Patch Set 2:

> I’m not sure how long the deletion will be completed. Will do some experiments.

Got 8 set of data, and the average is about 120 seconds.
Uploaded patch set 3.

Patch Set 3: Verified

Increased the maximum waiting time for wait_delete_completed in run_dd.sh and also the waiting time for background client load threads to start.

recovery-mds-scale test_failover_mds and test_failover_ost passed separately in 1 hour with MDS failed for 6 times and OST failed for 6 times:
 https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sets/24b0dc52-5da5-11e3-aed2-52540035b04c

The "run_dd_debug" logs showed that wait_delete_completed worked.

The above comments are just for information because we'd like not to use this solution.

Patch Set 3:

Build Started http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/20070/

Patch Set 3: Verified

Build Successful 
 
http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/20070/ : SUCCESS

Patch Set 3:

Rosso Autotest has started the test process

Patch Set 3:

Rosso Autotest has started the test process

Patch Set 3:

Tests received by maloo, run on CentOS release 6.4/x86_64: (https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/5e97fed0-5e97-11e3-b298-52540035b04c).  Ran 19 tests.  No failures.

Patch Set 3: Fails

Tests received by maloo, run on CentOS release 6.4/x86_64: (https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/f5c072d6-5eb2-11e3-a925-52540035b04c).  Ran 21 tests.  2 tests failed: sanity-quota, replay-ost-single.

Abandoned

Hongchao created a patch in http://review.whamcloud.com/#/c/8671/.

parents.subject: LU-4310 kernel: missed unlock in quota patch

commit.subject: LU-3326 tests: sync after file deleted in run_*.sh
commit.message:
LU-3326 tests: sync after file deleted in run_*.sh

In run_*.sh, call wait_delete_completed() after the file created
is deleted, then the dirty pages of the deleted file will be
flushed to the OST if MDT has not sent the object destroy request
to it.

Signed-off-by: Jian Yu <jian.yu@intel.com>
Change-Id: I37d0c457c3dd7a68b5ca5bd3504f358f6a356940
